Good afternoon, everyone. Thank you very much for having me here today. My name is Daniel, and I am a university professor and an institutional leader from Brazil. It is a real honor to be with you at this conference. I have worked in higher education for more than fifteen years, and my expertise is in international partnerships between universities. Over the years, I have represented my institution at meetings in many countries, and every single time, I learn something new. I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with all of you this week. I truly hope our conversations will lead to a productive partnership, and to a long collaboration between our institutions. Thank you again. I am very happy to be here, and I am happy to answer any questions.

Twenty lessons ago, I could not introduce myself in English. If someone asked me who I was, I went quiet, or I switched to Portuguese. Today is different. Today I can stand up at a conference in Seoul, look at the room, and say: good afternoon, my name is Maria Clara, and it is an honor to be here. I can say what I do. I can talk about my twenty years of experience, my two degrees, and my expertise. And I can say what I hope for: a productive partnership, an exchange of ideas, a real collaboration. My English is not perfect, and it does not need to be. It is calm, it is mine, and it is enough. After twenty lessons, I can present myself to the world, in English. And this time, I speak for my institution myself.
